verses.text: Job answers with sharp irony: 'no doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you,' yet he too has understanding. He resents their air of monopoly on truth. Self-appointed wisdom that looks down on others invites this just rebuke.

Job answers with sharp irony: 'no doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you,' yet he too has understanding. He resents their air of monopoly on truth. Self-appointed wisdom that looks down on others invites this just rebuke.

Job answers with sharp irony: 'no doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you,' yet he too has understanding. He resents their air of monopoly on truth. Self-appointed wisdom that looks down on others invites this just rebuke.

He is mocked though he called on God, the upright man laughed to scorn, while robbers' tents prosper. He points to a world that does not fit their tidy rule. The ease of the wicked and the grief of the godly confound every simple theory of reward.

Ask the beasts, birds, and fish, says Job; all know that the LORD's hand made them, and in His hand is the life of every creature. Creation itself preaches God's sovereignty. The humblest animal testifies that all life is held in His grip.

As the ear tests words and the mouth tastes food, so wisdom is found with the aged and length of days. Discernment is needed to weigh what is said. Job claims the right to sift his friends' arguments, not swallow them whole.

As the ear tests words and the mouth tastes food, so wisdom is found with the aged and length of days. Discernment is needed to weigh what is said. Job claims the right to sift his friends' arguments, not swallow them whole.

With God are wisdom, strength, and counsel; what He breaks down none can rebuild, and He leads counsellors away stripped. His power overrules all human cleverness. The mighty and the wise are alike in His hand, to raise or to bring low.

He leads princes away spoiled, overthrows the strong, looses the bonds of kings and girds them, and pours contempt on nobles. No rank is safe from His hand. The God who lifts up rulers can in a moment lay them in the dust.

He uncovers deep things out of darkness, enlarges nations and destroys them, and makes the chiefs of the earth wander in a pathless waste. His sovereignty governs the rise and fall of all. History is the unfolding of purposes only He can see.
